input:
The Prophet himself shared in the building work, joining his uncle, al-ʿAbbās, in his shifts. It is also said that when the men of the Quraysh met to start the demolition in order to rebuild the Kaʿbah, nobody was prepared to begin. They were extremely reluctant to pull the building down, fearing that something would happen to them. Al-Walīd ibn al-Mughīrah, one of their chiefs, volunteered to start. He took his axe and moved forward, repeating these words: “My Lord, we mean no offence.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 3: From Youth to Maturity

output.scene_title: Al-Walid Begins Demolition of the Ka'bah
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Chapter 3: From Youth to Maturity

characters.name: Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah
characters.appearance: Mid 40s, fair skin, athletic build. Thick black beard with slight wave, neatly trimmed. Wearing a long white thobe, plain dark brown leather cloak, dark woolen headwear wrapped over hair. Worn leather sandals. Confident, authoritative expression.

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Early 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a plain dark brown cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by dark brown cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Al-Abbas ibn Abd al-Muttalib
characters.appearance: Late 40s, dark brown skin, sturdy build. Thick black beard with slight grey streaks, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Kind, wise eyes, slightly stooped shoulders.

output.moral_lesson: Sometimes, taking the first step towards a difficult task is the bravest and most rewarding thing to do.
output.narrative_text: It was time to rebuild the Ka'bah, but nobody wanted to start. Al-Walid ibn al-Mughirah, a leader of the Quraysh, took the first step and began demolition with his axe, saying 'My Lord, we mean no offence.'

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, backlit by sunlight. Al-Walid and al-Abbas shown with full faces. Scene depicts a moment of hesitation and then action, suitable for children.

output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor illustration. Exterior of the old Ka'bah in Makkah. A man in off-white thobe and brown cloak, brown headwear, standing in front of the building with a large axe in hand, looking up towards the sky. Behind him, another man in simple white thobe, dark brown cloak, white turban, standing with eyes cast down, not contributing to the demolition. A third figure, a middle-aged man in white thobe and green cloak, white turban, seen entirely from behind, standing at a distance, observing the scene. Warm golden light, medium shot, eye level. Warm beige and brown palette. No modern objects.
